signals.title: GBP/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Fed Hike Doubt
signals.text: GBP/USD closed Friday at 1.3350 as weaker-than-expected US Nonfarm Payrolls dial back Fed rate hike expectations and fuel a broad USD selloff. This macro shift aligns with a strong G10 bilateral theme where GBP leads G10 performance (+0.759% 1W average across 4 pairs) and USD lags (-0.718% 1W average across 7 pairs), creating a 1.477% performance spread. The next Fed meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 29 July is the catalyst for confirming whether this policy divergence and USD weakness persist.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Crowded Short Squeeze Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY closed Friday at 161.337 with a strong options put skew of -1.67, signaling elevated hedging demand for downside protection. JPY speculative positioning is heavily crowded short with a net position of -115,033 contracts, leaving the pair highly vulnerable to a rapid short-squeeze unwind if US yields continue to soften. The next BoJ meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 31 July is the catalyst for resolving whether JPY's positive economic surprise index (net +12 beats) triggers a structural trend reversal.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Contrarian Reversal Risk
signals.text: USD/CAD closed Friday at 1.4198 amid an extreme retail sentiment imbalance where 93% of retail traders are short. This extreme positioning runs directly counter to a heavily crowded speculative short on CAD (net -87,096 contracts, 20.6% long) and a negative Canadian economic surprise index (net -6 misses), which support a higher USD/CAD bias. The next BoC meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 15 July is the catalyst for determining whether domestic data disappointments force a dovish shift that triggers a retail short squeeze.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Crowded Long Unwind Risk
signals.text: AUD/USD closed Friday at 0.6943 supported by a -69bp OIS carry advantage over the USD, but a crowded speculative net long of +29,035 contracts (64.9% long) creates unwind risk if global risk sentiment sours. While the RBA remains on hold at 4.35% with a mildly restrictive real rate of +0.39%, the options market remains cautious with a moderate put skew of -0.78. The next RBA meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 11 August is the catalyst for confirming whether the central bank maintains its restrictive policy stance.
